taxonomy.description: Playbooks group by the character of the goal they achieve, not by who runs them. A retriever picks one or two categories from this taxonomy, then drills into that category's INDEX.xml to find a specific playbook.

decision-tree.description: Read top-to-bottom. The first matching trigger wins. Triggers are written from the user's perspective — "I want to / I need to" framing.
decision-tree.step: If user says "is X worth doing?" / "should we even build this?" → discover-validate
If user has validated X and needs to design / spec it → plan-design
If user has a plan and needs to actually ship / produce → build-ship
If user has shipped X and it's now an ongoing rhythm (daily / weekly / quarterly) → operate-ritual
If user needs to make a single decision (A vs B, hire vs not, kill vs continue) → govern-decide
If something is broken / failing right now → fix-incident
If X works but should be cheaper / faster / cleaner → optimize-tune
If user needs to move from old-tech / old-process to new → migrate-rebuild
If user needs more users / leads / MRR → acquire-grow
If user needs to add a person / vendor / contractor → hire-onboard
If user needs to prove conformance (SOC2 / GDPR / accessibility / SLA) → audit-comply

category.trigger: є гіпотеза, треба перевірити чи варта роботи
category.intent: Turn an idea or assumption into evidence. Output is a written go/no-go decision backed by data.
category.scope: Customer interviews, problem validation, MVP tests, demand probes, kill criteria, willingness-to-pay tests.
category.typical-outputs: signed problem statement, kill criteria, willingness-to-pay evidence, validated MVP scope

category.trigger: є валідація, не знаю як саме будувати
category.intent: Turn validated intent into a buildable spec. Output is something a developer / designer / agent can execute against.
category.scope: SDD specs, design briefs, RFCs, ADRs, system designs, capacity models, threat models, test plans.
category.typical-outputs: spec.md, design.md, ADR pack, threat model, test plan, capacity model

category.trigger: є план, треба зробити і випустити
category.intent: Convert spec into deployed, working artefact. Output is a thing users can use.
category.scope: Feature delivery, MVP launches, content production, deploys, releases, prod cutover.
category.typical-outputs: deployed feature, PR merged, content live, release cut, prod traffic switched

category.intent: Run a recurring rhythm reliably. Output is the same kind of artefact week after week.
category.scope: Daily standups, weekly metrics reviews, sprint planning, quarterly OKR resets, monthly retros, on-call handoffs.
category.typical-outputs: weekly metrics report, sprint plan, OKR refresh, retro notes, on-call handoff log

category.trigger: треба прийняти одне рішення серед варіантів
category.intent: Gather inputs and make a single explicit decision. Output is a written decision with reasoning.
category.scope: Build vs buy, kill vs continue, A vs B technology choice, scope-in / scope-out, hire vs not, vendor evaluation gates.
category.typical-outputs: signed decision memo, ADR, vendor evaluation matrix, kill memo, scope gate sign-off

category.intent: Restore a broken thing to working state. Output is service / build / pipeline back to green.
category.scope: Production incidents, broken builds, failed deploys, security breaches, account lockouts, escalations.
category.typical-outputs: incident resolved, postmortem written, root cause logged, follow-up tickets created

category.intent: Improve a working thing along one axis (cost / speed / quality / reliability). Output is a measurable delta.
category.scope: Performance tuning, cost-cutting sweeps, conversion-rate experiments, latency reductions, code refactors, test-suite cleanups.
category.typical-outputs: perf delta, cost saving %, CR uplift, p95 improvement, refactor PR shipped

category.trigger: треба перейти зі старого на нове
category.intent: Move from one technology / process / contract to another with continuity. Output is the cutover completed cleanly.
category.scope: Framework upgrades, monolith→service splits, vendor switches, process change rollouts, schema migrations, repo moves.
category.typical-outputs: cutover plan executed, old tech retired, parallel-run period closed, rollback path tested

category.intent: Increase top-of-funnel or move users along the funnel. Output is a measurable funnel improvement.
category.scope: SEO campaigns, paid ads, content cadence, launch campaigns, PR pushes, referral programs, retention drips.
category.typical-outputs: +N users, +X MRR, +Y% conversion, launch executed, audience grown

category.intent: Add a person to the team and get them productive. Output is the person delivering useful work.
category.scope: First-hire, contractor onboarding, vendor selection, role definition, onboarding plans, 30-60-90 plans.
category.typical-outputs: signed contract, onboarding plan executed, first deliverable shipped, 90-day review

category.trigger: треба показати що ми відповідаємо вимогам
category.intent: Demonstrate conformance to an external standard. Output is an audit-ready evidence pack.
category.scope: SOC2, GDPR, accessibility, security reviews, SLA reporting, regulatory KYC, ISO27k mapping.
category.typical-outputs: evidence pack, control map, audit response binder, conformance attestation
